panels.description: Request success rate. Healthy above 99.5% (green), risky below 98% (red). Counts only normal completions with finished_reason='stop' as success.
panels.title: Success Rate

panels.description: Current number of running plus waiting requests. A sharp increase usually signals a traffic surge.
panels.title: Active Requests

panels.description: Scheduler efficiency: Running/(Running+Waiting). 100% means all requests are handled immediately. Below 70% suggests overload.
panels.title: System Efficiency

panels.description: P99 time to first token. A key indicator of perceived initial responsiveness. Under 1 second is ideal.
panels.title: TTFT P99

panels.description: [Critical] Preemption rate. Requests are interrupted because KV cache is exhausted. Anything above 0 needs immediate attention. Consider more GPU memory or a smaller batch size.
panels.title: Preemption Rate

panels.description: End-to-end time from request arrival to response completion. P50 covers half of requests, P99 covers 99%. A spike in P99 usually means a bottleneck needs investigation.
panels.title: E2E Request Latency

panels.description: Relative latency share of prefill (input processing) versus decode (output generation). High prefill suggests long prompts; high decode suggests long generations are the bottleneck.
panels.title: Inference Stage Breakdown

panels.description: Comparison of TTFT (time to first token) versus TPOT (time per output token). High TTFT points to a prefill bottleneck; high TPOT points to a decode bottleneck.
panels.title: TTFT vs TPOT

panels.description: Tokens processed per second. Input (prompt) means input tokens, Output (generation) means generated tokens. Higher throughput generally means better system efficiency.
panels.title: Token Throughput

panels.description: Ratio of input tokens to output tokens. Higher values suggest long inputs with short outputs (RAG); lower values suggest short inputs with long outputs (generation or summarization).
panels.title: Token I/O Ratio

panels.description: Percentage of work saved by prefix cache. Higher is better because more prefill work is skipped, saving GPU resources. Computed as (1 - actual work / total input) * 100.
panels.title: Prefix Cache Savings

panels.description: Distribution of input prompt token lengths by request. Darker colors mean more requests at that length. If traffic clusters in a specific range, consider optimizing for that range.
panels.title: Request Length Heatmap (Prompt)

panels.description: Time requests spend waiting in the queue. Queue time rises when GPUs are busy. If it stays high, consider scaling out.
panels.title: Request Queue Time

panels.description: Rolling 24h total input and output tokens for the selected model and service.
panels.title: 24h Token 总数

panels.description: Calendar-day token buckets. D-6 through D-1 are complete local calendar days ending at today 00:00; Today is from today 00:00 to now.
panels.title: 7天 Input/Output Token 统计（自然日）

panels.description: GPU KV cache usage. This memory stores intermediate attention state. Above 80% is a warning; above 95% risks preemption.
panels.title: GPU KV Cache Usage

panels.description: Prefix cache hit rate. Measures reuse of identical system prompts or few-shot examples. Above 70% is efficient and especially important in RAG workloads.
panels.title: Prefix Cache Hit Rate [5m]

panels.description: Request count by scheduler state. Running means currently executing on GPU, Waiting means queued, Swapped means moved out of memory. A steadily rising waiting count suggests overload.
panels.title: Scheduler State

panels.description: External prefix cache hit rate from KV connector cross-instance cache sharing. Measures how often external KV cache lookups succeed. Above 30% indicates effective cross-instance cache sharing.
panels.title: External Prefix Cache Hit Rate [5m]

panels.description: Combined prefix cache hit rate including both local and external (cross-instance) cache hits. External cache compensates for local cache misses, so Total = (local_hits + external_hits) / total_prompt_tokens. Above 70% is efficient.
panels.title: Total Prefix Cache Hit Rate [5m]

panels.description: Prefix cache hit rate (15m window). Measures reuse of identical system prompts or few-shot examples. Above 70% is efficient and especially important in RAG workloads.
panels.title: Prefix Cache Hit Rate [15m]

panels.description: External prefix cache hit rate (15m window) from KV connector cross-instance cache sharing. Measures how often external KV cache lookups succeed. Above 30% indicates effective cross-instance cache sharing.
panels.title: External Prefix Cache Hit Rate [15m]

panels.description: Combined prefix cache hit rate (15m window) including both local and external (cross-instance) cache hits. External cache compensates for local cache misses, so Total = (local_hits + external_hits) / total_prompt_tokens. Above 70% is efficient.
panels.title: Total Prefix Cache Hit Rate [15m]

panels.description: Python GC frequency and process memory usage. Frequent GC or steadily rising RSS may indicate a memory leak.
panels.title: Python GC & Memory

panels.description: Distribution of request finish reasons. stop means normal EOS completion, length means max length reached, abort means interrupted. A high abort rate is a problem.
panels.title: Finish Reason Distribution

panels.description: Total request throughput versus successful request throughput. A gap means some requests are failing. They should track closely under normal operation.
panels.title: Throughput vs Success

panels.description: MTP (Multi-Token Prediction) speculative decoding accept rate. Measures the fraction of generated output tokens that came from the draft model via speculative decoding. Above 50% means the draft model effectively accelerates generation. Computed as accepted_tokens / total_generation_tokens.
panels.title: MTP Accepted Token Ratio [5m]

panels.description: Accepted tokens per second by draft position (0-4). Position 0 is the first draft token (highest acceptance), position 4 is the last (lowest acceptance). Higher values at later positions indicate a stronger draft model. Stacked view shows total speculative throughput.
panels.title: MTP Accepted Tokens by Position

panels.description: Current accepted token rate per draft position. A bar gauge showing the instantaneous distribution of accepted tokens across draft positions (0-4). Steeper drop-off means the draft model loses accuracy quickly.
panels.title: MTP Draft Position Acceptance

panels.description: Conditional acceptance rate per draft position. Each position's acceptance rate relative to position 0 (baseline = 100%). Position 0 is always 100%; a steeper drop-off across higher positions means the draft model loses accuracy quickly. Above 50% at position 3+ indicates strong draft quality.
panels.title: MTP Accept Rate by Position
